prompt: Summarize the key obligations, term, termination triggers, and liability cap in this SaaS Master Services Agreement: 'Vendor shall provide the Hosted Service with 99.9% uptime measured monthly excluding scheduled maintenance. Term is 36 months from Effective Date, auto-renewing for 12-month periods unless either party gives 60 days written notice. Either party may terminate for material breach uncured after 30 days written notice. Vendor's total liability is capped at fees paid in the trailing 12 months, excluding indemnity for IP infringement and breach of confidentiality.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability cap: 'Customer pays $48,000 annually in advance. The Service is provided 'as is'; Vendor disclaims all implied warranties. Term: 24 months, no auto-renew. Either party may terminate for convenience with 90 days notice after month 12. Vendor's liability is capped at 6 months fees. Force majeure events suspend obligations for up to 60 days, after which either party may terminate.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, liability cap: 'Provider grants Customer a non-exclusive license to the Platform during the Subscription Term (initial 12 months, auto-renews monthly). Customer may terminate any auto-renewal with 15 days notice. Provider may terminate immediately for non-payment after 10 days written demand. Liability cap: greater of (a) 12 months fees or (b) $100,000. Carve-outs: indemnification, breach of Section 9 (Confidentiality), and gross negligence.'

Summarize the recipient's obligations, term, and survival clauses: 'Recipient shall hold Confidential Information in strict confidence for a period of 5 years from disclosure, using at least the same degree of care as for its own confidential information, and shall not disclose it to any third party except employees with a need-to-know who are bound by equivalent obligations. Obligations survive termination of the underlying business relationship.'

Summarize obligations, term, and survival: 'Receiving Party agrees to: (i) protect Disclosing Party's Confidential Information using reasonable measures; (ii) limit access to personnel with a legitimate need; (iii) not use such information for any purpose outside this engagement. Confidentiality obligations survive for 3 years post-termination, except trade secrets which survive indefinitely.'

Summarize obligations and survival: 'Each party (as Recipient) shall protect the other party's (as Discloser) Confidential Information for a period of 7 years following termination of this Agreement. Confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that is or becomes publicly known through no fault of Recipient, was rightfully known prior to disclosure, or is independently developed.'

Summarize the employee's key obligations, IP assignment, non-compete, and termination clauses: 'Employee assigns to Company all Inventions conceived during employment that relate to Company's business or are made using Company resources. Employee shall not, for 12 months post-termination, solicit Company's customers within North America. Either party may terminate at will with 2 weeks notice. Severance: 4 weeks pay if terminated without cause.'

Summarize obligations, IP, non-compete, termination: 'All work product created within scope of employment is work-for-hire and owned by Employer. Employee shall not engage in competing business activity during employment. Employer may terminate immediately for cause (defined as: fraud, gross negligence, material breach of policy). At-will employment otherwise. No severance unless terminated as part of a reduction in force.'

Summarize obligations, IP, non-compete, termination: 'Employee hereby assigns all right, title and interest in Inventions, whether patentable or not, conceived during the term of employment that (a) relate to the Company's business, or (b) result from work performed for Company. Non-solicitation period: 18 months. Employee may not solicit Company employees or contractors. Termination requires 30 days notice from either party.'

Summarize the IP licensing terms, royalty, and termination triggers: 'Licensor grants Licensee a worldwide, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use the Patented Technology in Field of Use (defined as: industrial water treatment, excluding municipal). Royalty: 4% of Net Sales, paid quarterly, with $50,000 annual minimum. Either party may terminate for material breach uncured after 60 days. Licensor may terminate immediately upon Licensee's bankruptcy or assignment for benefit of creditors.'

Summarize the obligations, term, termination, and liability cap: 'Vendor will provide 99.5% availability of the Service, excluding scheduled maintenance. Term is 24 months from the Effective Date, with automatic renewal for 12-month periods unless terminated by one party with 90 days written notice. Termination for cause is allowed after 30 days written notice of uncured breach. Vendor's total liability is limited to the amount paid in the last 12 months.'

Summarize the key obligations, term, termination triggers, and liability cap: 'Vendor shall provide the Service with a minimum 99% availability, except during scheduled downtime. The initial term is 36 months, renewable for successive 12-month periods unless one party provides 60-day notice. Either party may terminate for uncured breach after 30 days written notice. Vendor's liability is capped at the fees paid in the preceding 12 months.'

Summarize the obligations, term, termination, and liability cap: 'Customer pays $72,000 annually in advance. The Service is provided 'as is' with no warranties. Term: 36 months, no auto-renewal. Either party may terminate for convenience with 180 days notice after month 24. Vendor's liability is limited to 12 months' fees.'

Summarize the obligations, term, termination, and liability cap: 'Provider grants a non- exclusive license to the Software for the Subscription Term (initial 24 months, auto-renews monthly). Provider may terminate immediately for non-payment after 30 days written demand. Customer may terminate any auto-renewal with 30 days notice. Vendor's liability is capped at the greater of 12 months' fees or $50,000.'

Summarize the recipient's obligations, term, and survival clauses: 'Recipient must protect Discloser's Confidential Information using reasonable measures for 4 years from disclosure. Confidentiality obligations survive termination, except for trade secrets which are perpetual.'

Summarize obligations, term, and survival: 'Receiving Party must protect Disclosing Party's Confidential Information for 5 years post-disclosure. Obligations survive termination, except for trade secrets which are perpetual. Non-solicitation period: 24 months.'

Summarize obligations and survival: 'Each party (as Recipient) shall protect the other party's (as Discloser) Confidential Information for 6 years following the Agreement. Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability: 'Employee shall hold Company confidential information in strict confidence during and for 5 years after employment, and shall not disclose trade secrets. Base salary is $145,000 annually, paid bi-weekly. Employment is at-will; either party may terminate with 14 days written notice. Employee assigns all work product and inventions to Company. Severance: 4 weeks pay if terminated without cause.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability: 'Recipient shall use Discloser's Confidential Information solely to evaluate the hosted service integration. Confidentiality obligations survive for 3 years after termination. The Agreement term is 24 months. Either party may terminate for convenience with 30 days notice. Recipient's liability for unauthorized disclosure is capped at $250,000. Carve-outs: information that is publicly available or independently developed.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability: 'Provider personnel shall deliver the Hosted Platform with 99.5% monthly uptime. Customer pays $72,000 annually in advance. Term is 36 months, auto-renewing for 12-month periods unless either party gives 90 days notice. Provider assigns to Customer all work product created in custom integrations. Liability is capped at 12 months fees, excluding indemnity for IP infringement.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability: 'Employee must maintain a 95% on-time delivery metric measured monthly. Base salary is $120,000 annually. The initial term is 12 months, auto-renewing for 12-month periods unless either party gives 60 days notice. Employment is at-will. Employee assigns all inventions to Company. Severance: 6 weeks pay if terminated without cause.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability: 'Each employee of Recipient who accesses Confidential Information shall be bound by this NDA. Confidentiality survives 4 years post-termination. The Agreement term is 18 months. Either party may terminate with 30 days notice. Recipient shall not solicit Discloser's staff for 12 months. Liability for breach is capped at $500,000. Carve-outs: court-ordered disclosure and independently developed information.'

Summarize obligations, term, termination, and liability: 'Vendor provides the Hosted Service with 99.9% uptime measured monthly. Customer pays $96,000 annually. Both parties shall hold each other's Confidential Information in strict confidence for 5 years. Term is 24 months, auto-renewing for 12-month periods unless 60 days notice is given. Liability is capped at fees paid in the trailing 12 months, excluding breach of confidentiality and IP indemnity.'
